steps.title: Identify the logical relationship
steps.content: The blank connects two sentences: the first describes Ahoy's naval service, the second says he earned a place in history. The relationship is cause-and-effect — the recognition resulted from the service. The transition must signal that the second event came through or by means of the first.

steps.title: Eliminate wrong choices and confirm the answer
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘In any case,’: Signals concession or dismissal — that the second claim is true regardless of some complication. No complication is raised here. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Finally,’: Signals the last item in a sequence or list. The second sentence is not a final step; it is a result. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘For instance,’: Signals that what follows is a specific example of a general trend stated before. No general trend is stated. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘In doing so,’: Signals that through the action just described (naval service), the outcome (earning a place in history) was achieved. Fits the cause-and-effect relationship exactly. ✓
That confirms D is correct.
